nodes.name: When clicking 'Execute workflow'
nodes.notes: Stand-in for whatever actually triggers your bulk-delete workflow (a schedule, a webhook, an agent tool call).

nodes.name: Sample rows to delete
nodes.notes: Stand-in for the real upstream data (e.g. a query that found 3 stale customer rows). Replace with your real data source.

nodes.name: Reeflex Gate
nodes.notes: Requires the n8n-nodes-reeflex package installed and a 'Reeflex Core API' credential configured (Core URL + API Token). See the package README.

nodes.name: Execute Delete (Allowed)
nodes.notes: PLACEHOLDER - not a real delete. Replace with your real Postgres/WordPress/S3/etc. delete node. Only reached when reeflex-core returned decision=allow.

nodes.name: Notify approver (replace with Slack/Email)
nodes.notes: PLACEHOLDER - replace with a real Slack/Email/ticketing node. Include {{$json.reeflex.reason}}, {{$json.reeflex.hold_id}}, and {{$json.reeflex.expires_ts}} in the message so the approver has context.

nodes.name: Wait for approval
nodes.notes: Pauses this execution and exposes a resume webhook URL (see the node's 'Webhooks' tab after import). Your approval procedure - or the resolve step below, chained after your own external approval signal - calls that URL to resume. See /docs/guides/n8n.md for the full pattern, including reeflex-core's optional REEFLEX_WEBHOOK_URL (hold.created) as the trigger for a SEPARATE approval workflow when one core instance serves many concurrent holds.

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ { decision: "approve", principal: { type: "automation", id: "n8n-bulk-delete-guard-example" }, reason: "approved via n8n example workflow" } }}

nodes.name: Resolve hold
nodes.notes: Records the approval on reeflex-core (POST /v1/holds/{id}/resolve, 'automation' principal). This alone does NOT re-run the guarded action - see the next node.

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ {...$('Reeflex Gate').item.json.reeflex.envelope, approval: { present: true, hold_id: $('Reeflex Gate').item.json.reeflex.hold_id } } }}

nodes.name: Resubmit to /v1/decide
nodes.notes: Reuses reeflex.envelope from the Reeflex Gate node verbatim (action/axes/magnitude/target byte-identical, per SPEC hash binding), just flips approval.present/hold_id. Returns allow if the hold was approved and hasn't expired/been consumed; deny otherwise (reeflex_hold_not_approved / reeflex_hold_expired / reeflex_hold_consumed - see reeflex-core README).

nodes.name: Resubmission approved?
nodes.notes: Never assume the resubmission succeeded. A resubmission can still come back deny (hold expired/rejected/already consumed) - always check the response, exactly like the first /v1/decide call.

nodes.name: Execute Delete (after approval)
nodes.notes: PLACEHOLDER - not a real delete. Replace with the SAME real delete node used on the direct-Allowed path above.

nodes.name: Denied / rejected alert
nodes.notes: PLACEHOLDER - replace with a real alert node. Reached on a direct deny from Reeflex Gate, OR on a rejected/expired/already-consumed resubmission.

meta.description: Reeflex n8n community node example: PART A demo referenced from n8n-nodes-reeflex/README.md. Simulates a bulk-delete step gated by the Reeflex Gate node. Allowed -> placeholder delete. Held for Approval -> notify, Wait node (resume on webhook), resolve the hold (POST /v1/holds/{id}/resolve, automation principal), resubmit the SAME envelope to /v1/decide with approval.present=true, then branch again on the result. Denied (direct or post-resubmission) -> placeholder alert. Every PLACEHOLDER node is a no-op by design - wire your real nodes in before running this against a real backend.
